children.heading: Information sharing to facilitate reports and analysis
children.label: a Information sharing to facilitate reports and analysis

children.text: any information needed by CCHT to develop the strategy and proposal required under section 4(a); and1See References in Text note below.1
any additional data analysis to help CCHT better understand the issues surrounding human trafficking.

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: b Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the CCHT Director shall submit a report to Congress that identifies any legislation that is needed to facilitate the Department of Homeland Security’s mission to end human trafficking.December 27, 2022

children.heading: Annual report on potential human trafficking victims
children.label: c Annual report on potential human trafficking victims

children.text: the numbers of screened and identified potential victims of trafficking (as defined in ) at or near the international border between the United States and Mexico, including a summary of the age ranges of such victims and their countries of origin; andsection 7102(17) of title 22

an update on the Department of Homeland Security’s efforts to establish protocols and methods for personnel to report human trafficking, pursuant to the Department of Homeland Security Strategy to Combat Human Trafficking, the Importation of Goods Produced with Forced Labor, and Child Sexual Exploitation, published in January 2020.
